description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a prominent covered porch extending across the front, supported by several columns. The exterior is clad in horizontal siding, primarily in shades of gray, with large rectangular windows arranged in a regular pattern on both floors. The roof is a combination of a dark gable over the porch and a flat or low-pitched section over the main structure. The house features a spacious, open porch area with visible outdoor furniture, suggesting a focus on outdoor living. The rear and side elevations are simpler, with fewer architectural details and a more utilitarian appearance. The overall design is clean and symmetrical, with a modern take on traditional American suburban architecture.
description: A two-story gray house with a large covered front porch, horizontal siding, and regularly spaced rectangular windows. The design blends modern and traditional suburban elements with a prominent gable roof over the porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large covered porch, multiple columns, and main entry area with outdoor furniture.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rectangular window arrangement and the depth of the house, with the porch roof visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat or low-pitched roof section and a simpler window arrangement, with minimal decorative features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the porch's extension and the transition between the gable and flat roof sections.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered porch with multiple columns, steps leading up, and a simple door design.
